01BA:A676.label: xms_move_block
01BA:A676.comment: Copy words to/from XMS via 286 LOADALL trick. DOSMID's template at A60E adds a 6-byte prefix before the standard 286 layout, so all Intel-documented offsets are shifted +6 (ES cache at 0x36 instead of 0x30, DS cache at 0x48 instead of 0x42). LOADALL sets ES selector=0x8000 with cache.base from [A644] (rep movsw destination), DS selector=0x8000 with cache.base from [A656] (rep movsw source). For XMS writes: A644=0x110000+ (XMS), A656=0x02303A (conventional). For XMS reads: A644=conventional, A656=0x11xxxx (XMS). ES==DS selector collision is intentional; only the hidden caches differ.

01BA:A7BE.label: rotate_descriptor_words
01BA:A7BE.comment: Left-rotate 3 words at [BX]: new[0]=old[2], new[2]=old[4], new[4]=old[0]. Converts descriptor from (limit_hi+access, base_lo+base_mid, base_hi+limit_lo) storage format to the 286 LOADALL format (base_lo, base_mid, base_hi, limit_lo, limit_hi, access).

FDC8:44A6.label: set_xms_lock_flag
FDC8:44A6.comment: Loads DS from [FDC8:3DE7] (=0x0116), sets [0x0116:0323]=1, then STCs before returning. Called from xms_stub_fail (FDC8:44B7) to record a failed XMS lock attempt. The carry flag set here propagates through retf to callers.

FDC8:44B7.label: xms_stub_fail
FDC8:44B7.comment: Far-entry stub that signals failure: calls set_xms_lock_flag (sets [0116:0323]=1, CF=1), then retf. Reached via far pointer at [0116:00C8] (offset 0x44B7). Called once from FDC8:6B4C. Contrast with xms_stub_success (FDC8:44BB) which always returns CF=0.

FDC8:44BB.label: xms_stub_success
FDC8:44BB.comment: Far-entry no-op stub: CLC; RETF. Always returns CF=0 (success) without any side effect. All three far vectors [0116:0094], [0116:00B0], and [0116:00C0] point here (offset 0x44BB, segment FDC8). 103 far calls total: 101 from xms_block_access hot path + 1 from B38B + 1 from FDC8:AFAB. Indicates the driver considers these XMS operations trivially satisfied.

FDC8:513A.label: irq_save
FDC8:513A.comment: Save interrupt state and enter critical section. PUSHF to snapshot IF; checks nesting counter [0116:0D0C]: if zero (not already in critical section), gap code (A6CE-equivalent) handles CLI+counter increment; always exits via PUSH CS + CALL IRET to restore flags on the iret trick path. Paired with irq_restore (515A). Called 105 times.

FDC8:515A.label: irq_restore
FDC8:515A.comment: Restore interrupt state and leave critical section. Mirror of irq_save (513A): PUSHF, check nesting counter [0116:0D0C], restore saved flags via PUSH CS + CALL IRET trick. Decrements nesting counter when leaving outermost critical section. Called 105 times.

FDC8:6B88.label: xms_block_move_once
FDC8:6B88.comment: Cold-path (1 call) wrapper: calls irq_save (513A), loads XMS handle from [0116:001A]=3 into CX, pushes CX, calls xms_block_access_once (B38B) → xms_stub_success (clc;retf), pops CX, checks CF via jae. On success calls irq_restore (515A). Called once from FDC8:6B3B during init/teardown sequence.

FDC8:B342.label: xms_block_access
FDC8:B342.comment: Hot-path (101 calls) XMS block access trampoline. Loads XMS handle [0116:001A]=3 into BX, pushes BX and AX, calls far [0116:00B0] → xms_stub_success (FDC8:44BB, clc;retf), pops both. Checks CF via jae; gap FDC8:B350 handles error. Called from func_FDC8_7681 once per XMS block transfer.

FDC8:B38B.label: xms_block_access_once
FDC8:B38B.comment: Cold-path (1 call) 2-instruction trampoline: CALL FAR [0116:0094] (→ xms_stub_success, FDC8:44BB, clc;retf); RET. Called from xms_block_move_once (6B88) with XMS handle CX=3 on stack. CF=0 on return. Identical pattern to the hot-path xms_block_access (B342) which uses far vector [0116:00B0] instead of [0116:0094]; both targets resolve to the same clc;retf stub.

FF6D:A4C0.label: xms_block_move
FF6D:A4C0.comment: Far entry for XMS block move in the FF6D driver. Allocates 18-byte frame, reads 32-bit byte count from ES:[SI] into [bp-0x04]:[bp-0x02], calls func_FF6D_A560 twice to resolve dest (ES:[SI+4]) and src (ES:[SI+0xA]) XMS handle+offset pairs to 24-bit physical addresses, halves the byte count to word count (shr/rcr at A507), calls far [A00E] to lock A20, calls xms_block_copy_loadall, then calls far [A012] to unlock. Returns AX=1 on success. Called 752 times.

FF6D:A560.label: xms_resolve_physical
FF6D:A560.comment: Resolve an XMS block descriptor to a 24-bit physical address. BX points to a 6-byte structure: [BX]=ptr to block control record, [BX+2]=byte offset within block, [BX+4]=block index or segment. If control ptr is null: physical = [BX+4]*16 + [BX+2] (real-mode segment:offset). If control ptr is non-null and bit 1 set: maps XMS handle to physical via block_base_in_KB*1024 + offset; validates space >= transfer count, increments lock count. Returns AX=physical addr low, DX=physical addr high byte (24-bit). Called 1504 times (twice per xms_block_move).

FF6D:A6A0.label: xms_block_copy_loadall
FF6D:A6A0.comment: Inner XMS block copy via 286 LOADALL. Reads dest/src 24-bit physical addresses from caller frame ([bp-0x0e]/[bp-0x0c] and [bp-0x12]/[bp-0x10]) and writes them into the static GDT template vars [A680]/[A682] (dest) and [A66E]/[A670] (src). Increments counter [A69E]; normally falls through to the gap at A6CE (286 descriptor-setup instructions) then rotates both GDT descriptors via rotate_descriptor_words. Loops: clamps chunk to 0x7FFF words, saves 51 BIOS GDT words from phys 0x800 to [A5D2], installs XMS transfer GDT from [A638], executes LOADALL (CS:IP → FF6E:A749), post-LOADALL code restores BIOS GDT and runs rep movsw, then advances addresses and subtracts transferred words from [bp-0x04]:[bp-0x02] until zero. Decrements [A69E] before return. Called 752 times. KNOWN BUG: post-LOADALL stub issues STI at FF6E:A758 then immediately executes rep movsw at A759. If a timer interrupt fires in that 1-instruction window, the INT 8 handler at 0575:3AF7 executes 'pop es; pop ds' (restoring both to 0x8000), which destroys the LOADALL-set DS/ES descriptor cache bases used by rep movsw for the XMS transfer. The corrupt rep movsw then copies to/from wrong addresses; in the observed trace ~50% of iterations are corrupted this way.

FF6D:A7E8.label: rotate_descriptor_words
FF6D:A7E8.comment: Left-rotate 3 words at [BX]: new[0]=old[2], new[2]=old[4], new[4]=old[0]. Identical to 01BA:A7BE. Converts a GDT descriptor from storage format to 286 LOADALL layout. Called twice per XMS transfer: first with BX=A692 (dest descriptor), then with BX=A686 (src descriptor).

0019:40EB.label: int_21h_dispatch
0019:40EB.comment: DOS kernel INT 21h entry point. Saves all regs, sets up DOS stack, dispatches on AH value, then restores regs and IRETs. Segment 0019 is the DOS kernel.

0019:4275.label: load_ds_si_from_stack_ptr
0019:4275.comment: Loads DS from [0x3DD7] and SI from DOS stack-top pointer at [0x0584]. Used as a helper to get the current task's data pointer.

0B73:0364.label: a20_after_xms
0B73:0364.comment: A20 gate management called after each XMS block transfer. Reads/writes port 0x92 to enable or disable A20. Segment 0B73 is the HIMEM.SYS / A20 controller. A20 is briefly enabled then immediately disabled during HIMEM init; the gate state during LOADALL transfers depends on HIMEM calling convention.

1036:1941.label: track_seek_sync
1036:1941.comment: If the dirty bit ([si+0x2E] bit 0) is set, issues DOS AH=42h (LSEEK) to [si+0x06]:[si+0x08], then clears the dirty bit. Called before every buffer refill.

1036:197F.label: track_seek
1036:197F.comment: Adjust track file position. DX=0: seek to start. DX=1: add signed 32-bit BX:CX to [si+0x06]:[si+0x08] (e.g. BX=CX=0xFFFF = seek -1 for running-status unget). DX=2: set position to BX:CX. Always sets dirty bit ([si+0x2E] |= 1) so track_seek_sync will re-lseek before next read.

1036:1A2A.label: track_buffer_refill
1036:1A2A.comment: Refills the 32-byte per-track file buffer. Calls track_seek_sync to sync the file pointer, then DOS AH=3Fh to read 32 bytes into [si+0x0A] (track data buffer). Updates [si+0x2A]:[si+0x2C] to the new buffer start position.

1036:1B09.label: track_read_bytes
1036:1B09.comment: [HOT] Buffered track byte reader. AX=track ptr, BX=dest offset, CX=dest segment, DX=count. Checks whether the requested bytes fall within the 32-byte buffer; if not, calls track_buffer_refill. Then copies count bytes from [si+0x0A + (pos - buf_start)] to BX:CX.

1036:1D76.label: schedule_midi_event_output
1036:1D76.comment: Schedule a MIDI event for output. AX=scheduler ptr (1BB5:9F28), DX:BX=segment:offset of event data, CX=segment for driver. Checks [0x15be]; if zero, calls func_1036_46D7 to dispatch via far-ptr driver at [0x9F44] with AH=0x0B.

1036:1DB7.label: midi_timing_scheduler
1036:1DB7.comment: Called once per MIDI event after processing. DX=0 and AX=0: mark song done and reset time. DX!=0: call func_1036_1E51 to compute elapsed time, then schedule event via func_1036_5609. Returns AX=0 normally, non-zero when song ends or transitions.

1036:1E51.label: calc_event_timer_ticks
1036:1E51.comment: Convert MIDI timing to emulator timer ticks. BX=number of MIDI timing units (e.g. 0x000E). Reads accumulated time from [0x15C0]:[0x15C2], adds BX scaled by tempo at [0x9F2C] (observed 0x00EF), clamps against [0x9F2C], stores result back. Returns DX:AX = new time value.

1036:1FDB.label: read_midi_delta
1036:1FDB.comment: Reads a MIDI variable-length quantity (VLQ) delta-time from the current track position. Returns the 32-bit delta value in DX:AX and advances [si+0x06]:[si+0x08]. Stores result in [bp-0x16]:[bp-0x14] for the caller.

1036:24E6.label: dispatch_midi_channel_msg
1036:24E6.comment: [HOT] Dispatch a MIDI channel message. BL=status byte, AX=scheduler ptr, DX=track ptr. Decodes command nibble (0xB0=control change, 0xC0=program change, 0xD0=aftertouch, 0xE0=pitch bend, 0x90=note on/off, 0xA0=poly aftertouch). Reads data bytes via track_read_bytes, stores into scheduler state, sets note bitmask for note-on. Called 525 times per song pass.

1036:2AD6.label: mpu401_wait_write_ready
1036:2AD6.comment: Spin until MPU-401 port 0x331 bit 6 (DRR) = 0 (output buffer empty, ready to accept data). DX=MPU-401 base port (0x330). Calls func_1036_2AC1 first, then polls status. With AL=0x3F (standard idle status), bit 6=0 so the loop exits immediately.

1036:2B2D.label: mpu401_check_data
1036:2B2D.comment: Check if MPU-401 has incoming data. Reads port AX+1 (0x331 status). Tests bit 7: if set (AL=0xBF = data in output buffer), branches to xor ax,ax / ret → returns 0. Always returns AX=0 during normal playback because the 0xFE UART-mode ACK stays in the MPU buffer unread.

1036:2B80.label: timer_wait_loop
1036:2B80.comment: [HOT] Wait until the 32-bit timer at [0x15E4] reaches a target value. Calls mpu401_check_data (returns 0 = no MPU event), then reads current timer via func_1036_3BE4, compares against [bp-0x02]:[bp-0x04] target. Loops ~9785 times across 527 MIDI events (average ~5 timer ticks per event at ~50 IRQ/s).

1036:2BBA.label: mpu401_send_uart_cmd
1036:2BBA.comment: Send MPU-401 enter-UART-mode command. DX=MPU-401 base port (0x330). Waits for write-ready via func_1036_2AD6, then writes 0x3F to port 0x331 (command port). The MPU queues 0xFE ACK in response.

1036:3B00.label: pit_timer_isr
1036:3B00.comment: [HOT] PIT channel-0 interrupt handler (INT 8). Adds 0x035A to 32-bit timer at [0x15E4]:[0x15E6], increments mod-64 counter at [0x15E8]. Every 64 ticks: calls func_1036_94AA to chain to original BIOS handler. Always sends EOI (OUT 0x20, 0x20) via func_1036_5AA1 before IRET. Fired ~50 times/sec (DOSMID reprograms PIT for finer resolution). 2563 invocations in 51-second trace.

0575:3AF7.label: timer_handler_xms_context
0575:3AF7.comment: INT 8 timer handler that fires during HIMEM.SYS LOADALL XMS transfers. Runs in segment 0575 (HIMEM.SYS or related driver). Pushes AX, CX, DX, BX, BX again, DX, CX, AX to save state; calls io_write_byte (1036:5AA1) with DX=0x0020 / AX=0x0020 to send PIC EOI; then pops AX, AX, ES, DS restoring ES=0x8000 and DS=0x8000 (their pre-interrupt values). CRITICAL: the pop es (0575:3B4B) and pop ds (0575:3B4C) reset es_cache.base and ds_cache.base to 0x8000*16=0x80000, destroying the LOADALL-set descriptor cache bases that rep movsw at FF6E:A759 relies on for the XMS transfer. After IRET to FF6E:A759, rep movsw copies from/to physical 0x80000 instead of the correct XMS addresses, silently corrupting the XMS data transfer.

1036:3BE4.label: read_timer_atomic
1036:3BE4.comment: [HOT] Atomically read the 32-bit timer [0x15E4]:[0x15E6] into [BX]:[BX+2]. Wraps with CLI/STI to prevent partial reads across a timer tick interrupt. Called 9787 times from the timer wait loop.

1036:3C41.label: screen_write_cell
1036:3C41.comment: [HOT] Write one character cell to the video buffer. AX=row, DX=column, BX=char+attribute word. Computes offset = (AX*80 + DX)*2, loads screen buffer far-ptr from [0x15EA] via LES, writes BX to ES:[SI+offset]. Called 3680 times — handles DOSMID's text-mode UI updates.

1036:46D7.label: midi_driver_dispatch
1036:46D7.comment: Dispatch one MIDI event through the output driver. AX=scheduler ptr (9F28), DX:BX=segment:offset of event struct, CX:pushedstack=segment for copy. Copies event data to a local frame, sets AH=0x0B, calls far [0x9F44] (driver far-ptr). Returns AX=1 on success; called 527 times per pass.

1036:5609.label: memcpy_es
1036:5609.comment: [HOT] Copy BX bytes from DS:DX to ES:DI. Uses rep movsw + rep movsb for odd bytes. Returns AX=original DI. Called 530 times as the scheduler's buffer-copy helper.

1036:5948.label: memcpy_with_seg
1036:5948.comment: [HOT] Copy word-count in CX bytes from CX:AX (segment:offset) to ES:BX. Saves/restores DS around a rep movsw + movsb. Returns AX=original BX (destination offset). Called 1850 times from track_read_bytes.

1036:5A99.label: io_read_byte
1036:5A99.comment: [HOT] Read one byte from I/O port AX (AX is converted to DX first). Returns AL=value, AH=0. Used exclusively to read MPU-401 status from port 0x331. Returns 0x3F (idle: bit6=0 ready-to-write, bit7=0 no-data) or 0xBF (data pending).

1036:5AA1.label: io_write_byte
1036:5AA1.comment: [HOT] Write AL=DL to I/O port AX. Used for MPU-401 data/command writes (port 0x330/0x331) and PIC EOI (OUT 0x20, 0x20). Called 2895 times.

1036:8E92.label: disable_interrupts
1036:8E92.comment: [HOT] CLI; RET. Wrapper used to atomically disable interrupts around timer reads. Called 9788 times.

1036:8E94.label: enable_interrupts
1036:8E94.comment: [HOT] STI; RET. Paired with disable_interrupts. Called 9788 times.

1036:94AA.label: chain_to_bios_timer
1036:94AA.comment: Chain INT 8 to the original BIOS handler. Called every 64 PIT ticks (41 times). Swaps AX/CX into the saved interrupt frame via XCHG, clears trap/overflow flags, then does POPF + RETF to transfer control to the saved CS:IP of the original timer vector.

lineComments.01BA:A683: swap new dest XMS addr (AX=low, DL=high) with static transfer state at [A656]/[A658]; A656 maps to DS cache (rep movsw source) in LOADALL table at offset 0x48
lineComments.01BA:A68D: swap new src XMS addr (CX=low, DH=high) with static transfer state at [A644]/[A646]; A644 maps to ES cache (rep movsw destination) in LOADALL table at offset 0x36
lineComments.01BA:A69D: inc transfer counter; if wrapped to 0, skip normal descriptor update
lineComments.01BA:A6C2: 0F 01 — LGDT-class instruction; installs GDT for upcoming LOADALL
lineComments.01BA:A6C5: 0F 01 E0 — SMSW AX: reads CR0 low 16 bits to detect protected mode (returns 0x0000 = real mode; LOADALL trick requires real mode)
lineComments.01BA:A6CC: rotate GDT descriptor words for dest at [A668]
lineComments.01BA:A6D5: rotate GDT descriptor words for src at [A65C]
lineComments.01BA:A6D8: clamp transfer count to max 0x7FFF words (INT 15h/87h limit)
lineComments.01BA:A6EB: GDT segment: physical 0x0080*16 = 0x800 (where LOADALL table lives)
lineComments.01BA:A6FB: save SP so XMS transfer can restore stack after LOADALL switch
lineComments.01BA:A700: hardcoded save of DS=1BB5 (caller's data segment)
lineComments.01BA:A70E: save current BIOS GDT area (102 bytes: DS:0000 → ES:A5A8) before overwriting with XMS transfer table
lineComments.01BA:A71B: install XMS transfer GDT (102 bytes: 01BA:A60E → 0080:DI) for LOADALL; template has 6-byte prefix shifting all standard 286 offsets by +6
lineComments.01BA:A71D: 0F 05 — 286 LOADALL: atomically loads ALL CPU state from phys 0x800. Sets CS:IP=01BB:A71F. Table offsets shifted +6 from Intel standard: ES cache at 0x36 (base from [A644]), DS cache at 0x48 (base from [A656]). First observed LOADALL: ES_cache.base=0x110000 (XMS write dest), DS_cache.base=0x02303A (conventional read src).
lineComments.01BB:A71F: GDT restore loop: CS:lodsw 51 times, writing back saved BIOS GDT words to [SS:BP] (= phys 0x800). Runs under the LOADALL-injected CS descriptor, then rep movsw transfers data using the LOADALL-set DS/ES caches.
lineComments.01BB:A728: mov ss, bx restores normal SS; the DS and ES caches (with XMS addresses) stay valid until an explicit segment load overwrites them
lineComments.01BB:A72F: rep movsw: actual XMS data transfer using LOADALL-set descriptor caches. Source=DS:SI (cache.base=DS phys from A656), Dest=ES:DI (cache.base=ES phys from A644). Both DS and ES selectors are 0x8000; the emulator must use the hidden caches, not selector*16.
lineComments.01BB:A731: far jmp back to 01BA to restore normal CS descriptor cache
lineComments.1036:1952: read current logical file pos from track struct
lineComments.1036:1958: AH=42h: DOS LSEEK command code stored in [bp-0x0E]
lineComments.1036:1B4A: check if current pos high word >= buffer-end high word (buffer miss test)
lineComments.1036:1B51: check if current pos low word >= buffer start (buffer miss test, low word)
lineComments.1036:2700: status byte (bit 7 set): save as running status in [bp-0x04]
lineComments.1036:2703: status byte path: jump directly to event processing (no seek-back needed)
lineComments.1036:2705: data byte (bit 7 clear): running status — seek back 1 byte so it's re-read as data for the current running status command
lineComments.1036:270F: call track_seek(-1): unget the data byte so the caller reads it again under the running status
lineComments.1036:2AE4: test bit 6 (DRR): 0=MPU ready to accept write; 0x3F & 0x40 = 0 so loop exits immediately on first check under normal conditions
lineComments.1036:2B31: test bit 7 of MPU-401 status (0x80): if set (=0xBF = data available), branch to ret AX=0; 0xBF is returned whenever the unread 0xFE ACK sits in the MPU output buffer
lineComments.1036:3B0B: add 0x035A (858) to 32-bit timer [0x15E4]:[0x15E6] each PIT tick; at ~50 IRQ/s accumulates ~42900/sec
lineComments.0575:3B41: mov dx, 0x0020 — load PIC1 command port for EOI
lineComments.0575:3B49: pop ax — restore saved AX (second pop)
lineComments.0575:3B4A: pop ax — restore saved AX (first pop)
lineComments.0575:3B4B: pop es — restores ES=0x8000; RESETS es_cache.base to 0x80000, destroying LOADALL-set XMS destination address
lineComments.0575:3B4C: pop ds — restores DS=0x8000; RESETS ds_cache.base to 0x80000, destroying LOADALL-set XMS source address
lineComments.0575:3B55: iret — returns to FF6E:A759; rep movsw will now copy from/to physical 0x80000 (wrong) instead of XMS addresses
lineComments.FF6D:A507: shr/rcr: divide 32-bit byte count by 2 to convert to word count for LOADALL transfers
lineComments.FF6D:A50F: far call to lock A20 gate (via far-ptr [A00E]) before LOADALL-based copy
lineComments.FF6D:A51C: far call to unlock A20 gate (via far-ptr [A012]) after copy completes
lineComments.FF6D:A6A1: load dest physical addr low word from caller frame into AX
lineComments.FF6D:A6A4: load dest physical addr high byte from frame into DL
lineComments.FF6D:A6A7: load src physical addr low word from frame into CX
lineComments.FF6D:A6AA: load src physical addr high byte from frame into DH
lineComments.FF6D:A6AD: swap dest addr low word (AX) with static [A680]; installs new dest base and discards old
lineComments.FF6D:A6B2: swap dest addr high byte (DL) with [A682]
lineComments.FF6D:A6B7: swap src addr low word (CX) with static [A66E]; installs new src base
lineComments.FF6D:A6BC: swap src addr high byte (DH) with [A670]
lineComments.FF6D:A6C7: inc transfer counter [A69E]; je at A6CC skips gap (descriptor setup) only on 65536-transfer wrap
lineComments.FF6D:A702: top of chunk loop; clamp this transfer to min(remaining, 0x7FFF) words
lineComments.FF6D:A718: BX=FF6D → current data segment (used to restore DS/ES after LOADALL)
lineComments.FF6D:A71A: DX=0x33=51 words — size of GDT save/restore area
lineComments.FF6D:A725: save SP into template so post-LOADALL code can restore the stack
lineComments.FF6D:A72F: store clamped word count for this chunk into template at [A668]
lineComments.FF6D:A738: rep movsw: save 51 BIOS GDT words from phys 0x800 (DS=0080:0) to FF6D:A5D2 (ES=FF6D)
lineComments.FF6D:A745: rep movsw: install 51-word XMS transfer GDT from FF6D:A638 (DS=FF6D) into phys 0x800 (ES=0080:0)
lineComments.FF6D:A747: 0F 05 — 286 LOADALL: loads all CPU state from phys 0x800. CS selector=0x0001 (table offset 0x22) but cs_cache.base=0xFF6E0 (from descriptor cache at 0x3C-0x41 after rotate_descriptor_words), so post-LOADALL code fetches from physical FF6E:A749 correctly. ES cache base = dest physical addr (increments each iteration), DS cache base = src physical addr (fixed at 0x003F82 for conventional memory read). FLAGS=0x0000 so interrupts are disabled (IF=0) during the GDT restore loop. IP=0xA749.
lineComments.FF6D:A775: return point after LOADALL (far jmp from FF6E:A75B restores normal CS)
lineComments.FF6D:A777: SI = bytes just transferred (from post-LOADALL movsw); shr converts to words
lineComments.FF6D:A77B: subtract words transferred from 32-bit remaining count [bp-0x04]:[bp-0x02]
lineComments.FF6D:A784: advance dest addr [A680]/[A682] by SI bytes transferred
lineComments.FF6D:A78F: advance src addr [A66E]/[A670] by DI bytes transferred
lineComments.FF6D:A7A3: LOADALL restores template area (covers [A69E]) resetting counter to 0x0000; je always taken in observed trace
lineComments.FF6D:A7E2: dec [A69E] (0x0000 → 0xFFFF) before ret; keeps counter in sync across calls

gaps.01BA:A6A4: normal-path descriptor updates; protected-mode instructions not decoded by disassembler
gaps.01BA:A74B: post-LOADALL return path: counts transferred words, decrements remaining count, loops or returns
gaps.FF6D:A6CE: descriptor setup: 286 protected-mode instructions (LGDT/LIDT) to update GDT entries; not decoded by disassembler; skipped on 65536-transfer counter wrap
gaps.FF6D:A749: post-LOADALL stub fully decoded (runs at FF6E under LOADALL-injected CS cache, CS selector=0x0001 but cs_cache.base=0xFF6E0 so physical fetch is correct): [A749] 2E AD lodsw (CS override, reads from CS:SI=FF6E:A5D2+n saving BIOS GDT words); [A74B] 89 46 00 mov [bp+0x00],ax (writes to SS:BP=0x0080:0=phys 0x800, restoring BIOS GDT table); [A74E-A74F] inc bp; inc bp; [A750] E2 F7 loop A749 (51 iterations, CX=0x33); [A752] 8E D3 mov ss,bx (restores SS=0x10F4); [A754] 8B CA mov cx,dx (CX=transfer word count, observed 7); [A756] 8B F7 mov si,di (SI=0); [A758] FB sti; [A759] F3 A5 rep movsw (XMS data copy using LOADALL-set ES/DS caches); [A75B] EA 75 A7 6D FF jmp far FF6D:A775. BUG: if timer fires between A758 (sti) and A759 (rep movsw), INT 8 at 0575:3AF7 corrupts DS/ES caches via pop es/pop ds restoring 0x8000; after IRET the byte at FF6E:A759 reads as 0xFB (sti, not F3) causing 7 sti nops until jmp far at A760 returns to FF6D:A775 with SI=0 (zero bytes transferred reported).
